meta.title: Tradux Examples & Integrations
meta.description: Browse Tradux live starters, docs, and clone-and-run commands for React, Vue, Svelte, Vanilla JS, and Astro with SSR-friendly language switching.

hero.title: Ship multilingual UI without starting from zero
hero.lead: Browse live Tradux starters, compare the framework integrations, and copy the exact clone-and-run commands you need. This root page now switches locale on the server, so the HTML itself arrives translated and accessible.

terminal.title: From install to translated UI
terminal.note: The examples follow the README path: install Tradux, initialize config, generate locales, then switch language from your app.
lines: npm install tradux && npm tradux init

summary.title: AI-powered CLI
summary.description: Initialize your config, translate JSON with your preferred provider, and keep the workflow close to your codebase.

summary.title: Differential updates
summary.description: Tradux remembers what changed, so update runs only retranslate the keys that actually moved.

summary.title: SSR-friendly cookies
summary.description: Server-rendered apps can read the tradux_lang cookie before rendering and ship the right locale immediately.

summary.title: Real starters
summary.description: Open a framework example, inspect the import path, and test the live route before cloning anything locally.

workflow.title: Translate JSON once, then let the runtime react everywhere
workflow.description: The landing page is aligned with the project README: CLI first, reactive runtime second, SSR support when your stack needs it.

steps.title: 1. Create source copy
steps.description: Keep a default language JSON as the source of truth and use special markers when some keys must stay untouched or always update.

steps.title: 2. Generate locales with the CLI
steps.description: Tradux translates the missing content, updates only what changed later, and keeps available languages synchronized with your files.

steps.title: 3. Render the right language
steps.description: Framework hooks keep SPAs reactive, while SSR apps can read the cookie first and send fully localized HTML from the server.

integrationsSection.title: Pick the starter that matches your stack
integrationsSection.description: Every card includes the live route, the right import path, a quick explanation of when to use it, and a richer command block you can copy.

card.commandMeta: Clone the monorepo, jump into the example, install, and run.

react.name: React + Vite
react.description: Use the React hook when you want reactive translations inside components with language switching triggered from your UI.
react.start: Clone the repository, enter the React example, install dependencies with pnpm, and start the Vite dev server.
react.command:
git clone https://github.com/JojoDeveloper01/Tradux.git
cd Tradux/examples/react-vite
pnpm install
pnpm dev

vue.name: Vue + Vite
vue.description: Use the Vue integration when you want Tradux state inside components through the framework composable API.
vue.start: Clone the repository, open the Vue example, install dependencies, and run the local Vite server.
vue.command:
git clone https://github.com/JojoDeveloper01/Tradux.git
cd Tradux/examples/vue-vite
pnpm install
pnpm dev

svelte.name: Svelte + Vite
svelte.description: Use the Svelte integration when you want Tradux values exposed as stores with automatic updates after a language change.
svelte.start: Clone the repository, move into the Svelte example, install packages, and start the Vite dev server.
svelte.command:
git clone https://github.com/JojoDeveloper01/Tradux.git
cd Tradux/examples/svelte-vite
pnpm install
pnpm dev

vanilla.name: Vanilla JS + Vite
vanilla.description: Use the base package when you are outside a framework and want to initialize Tradux directly with JavaScript or TypeScript.
vanilla.start: Clone the repository, open the Vanilla example, install dependencies, and run the Vite environment.
vanilla.command:
git clone https://github.com/JojoDeveloper01/Tradux.git
cd Tradux/examples/vanilla-vite
pnpm install
pnpm dev

astro.name: Astro
astro.description: Use the Astro example when you need server-rendered translations and want to read the Tradux cookie before sending HTML.
astro.start: Clone the repository, open the Astro example, install dependencies, and start the Astro dev server.
astro.command:
git clone https://github.com/JojoDeveloper01/Tradux.git
cd Tradux/examples/astro
pnpm install
pnpm dev
